description: Registry of FREE external tools and platforms agents should consider for implementations. Zero additional cost mandate.
costRule: ABSOLUTE: Only use tools with $0 cost. If a tool requires payment, subscription, or credits beyond free tier — DO NOT USE. Always verify free tier limits before recommending.

costBenefitGate.description: MANDATORY analysis before implementing ANY new tool, integration, or feature
steps: 1. COMPLEXITY: How hard to set up and maintain? (Low <1h / Medium 1-4h / High >4h)
2. COST: Is it $0 now AND forever? (Free / Free-with-limits / Paid=REJECT)
3. VALUE: What problem does it solve? How often does it help? (Daily/Weekly/Rare)
4. ALTERNATIVES: Can existing tools do this? (GitHub Actions, jq, bash, existing workflows)
5. VERDICT: Only implement if value > complexity AND cost = $0

decisionMatrix.highValue_highComplexity: EVALUATE — is there a simpler alternative?
decisionMatrix.lowValue_lowComplexity: MAYBE — only if trivial to add
decisionMatrix.anyCost_aboveZero: REJECT — find free alternative or skip

costBenefitGate.rule: If the answer to step 4 is YES (existing tools can do it), ALWAYS use existing tools instead of adding new ones. Simplicity beats features.

ai_assistants.description: AI platforms available for research, code generation, analysis — all free tier

items.name: Claude Web (claude.ai)
items.limits: Free tier: limited messages/day. Pro plan already active.
items.integration: Manual — copy context, get analysis, apply here
items.when: Complex architecture decisions, long document analysis, brainstorming approaches before implementing

items.name: Claude Code (CLI)
items.integration: Primary tool — this is where agents operate
items.when: All implementation work, deploys, fixes, monitoring

items.name: ChatGPT Free (chat.openai.com)
items.integration: Manual — use for second opinion when stuck
items.when: When Claude is unavailable or need a different perspective

items.name: GitHub Copilot (in IDE)
items.limits: Free tier for public repos + Copilot Free
items.when: During manual coding in IDE for autocomplete and suggestions

items.name: Google Gemini (gemini.google.com)
items.integration: Manual — good for searching current documentation
items.when: Need up-to-date info on tools/APIs, analyze screenshots of CI failures

code_quality.description: Free code analysis and quality tools

items.name: GitHub Actions
items.limits: 2000 min/month free for private repos, unlimited for public
items.when: All automation, monitoring, and scheduled tasks

items.name: GitHub Code Scanning (CodeQL)
items.when: Deep security analysis beyond our custom scans

items.name: Snyk (Free Tier)
items.when: Scanning Docker images, Terraform configs, npm packages for vulnerabilities

items.name: GitHub Pages
items.when: Dashboard, docs, any static web content

items.name: GitHub Wiki
items.when: Detailed operational docs that don't belong in repo files

items.name: GitHub Issues + Projects
items.integration: Native — our primary work tracking system

items.name: UptimeRobot (Free Tier)
items.when: Monitor dashboard (GitHub Pages) uptime and corporate endpoints

items.name: Grafana Cloud (Free Tier)
items.when: When we need visual dashboards beyond GitHub Pages

community_intelligence.description: Free knowledge sources agents should check for new patterns and best practices

items.name: anthropics/skills (GitHub)
items.when: Learning new agent patterns and capabilities

items.name: Stack Overflow / GitHub Discussions
items.integration: Manual — search when stuck on specific error
items.when: Debugging unfamiliar errors or tool-specific issues

beforeImplementing: Check if a FREE external tool already solves this problem
Check if GitHub-native features can handle it (Actions, Pages, Issues, Wiki, CodeQL, Dependabot)
Check community intelligence sources for existing patterns
If using Claude Web for research, summarize findings and codify in repo (GitHub-First)
costVerification: ALWAYS verify the tool is free before recommending
If free tier has limits, document the limits clearly
If approaching free tier limits, find alternative before hitting paywall
NEVER recommend tools that require credit card for free tier
integrationPriority: 1. GitHub-native (Actions, Pages, Issues, CodeQL, Dependabot) — already integrated, zero setup
2. CLI tools in workflows (jq, python, curl) — zero cost, runs in Actions
3. Free-tier SaaS with GitHub integration (Snyk, UptimeRobot) — easy setup
4. Manual AI assistants (Claude Web, ChatGPT, Gemini) — for research and second opinions
5. Self-hosted open-source (n8n, Grafana) — if infrastructure available
